chunk_summary: The persistent diarrhea diet ladder provides structured, timebound dietary interventions to manage persistent diarrhea, focusing on reducing lactose while maintaining energy density. It includes Diet A (low lactose), Diet B (lactose-free), and Diet C (glucose-based) with specific recipes and escalation protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the structured approach of the persistent diarrhea diet ladder for nutritional rehabilitation.
Learn the specific recipes and nutritional composition of Diet A, Diet B, and Diet C.
Identify clinical indicators for transitioning between diet stages and when to refer for advanced care.
Recognize the importance of maintaining energy density while managing lactose intolerance in persistent diarrhea.
key_facts: Diet A (low lactose): 96 kcal/100 g; recipe includes milk 40 mL, sugar 2.25 g, puffed rice powder 12.5 g, oil 2 g, and water to 100 mL.
Diet B (lactose-free): 92.2 kcal/100 g; recipe includes puffed rice 13.5 g, egg white 11 g, sugar/glucose 3.5 g, oil 3.5 mL, and water to 100 mL.
Diet C: Glucose-based with protein (egg white, chicken, or protein hydrolysate) and added oil for energy density.
Each diet step is trialed for ~7 days with monitoring of stool frequency/consistency, perianal excoriation, hydration, and weight trajectory.
Breastfeeding should be continued throughout the protocol.
Refractory cases or those with severe undernutrition may require referral for total parenteral nutrition.
reference_quote: The IAP persistent diarrhea guideline provides a diet ladder that can be written as timebound prescriptions and prevents two common harms: fasting and repeated formula switching without assessment.
document_key_topics: Escalation to advanced care in refractory cases
